central_research_question: What policy interventions would most efficiently close Canada's research-to-commercialization gap in AI?
client: Government of Canada — Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED) and Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat

recommendations.title: National Responsible AI Governance and Adoption Program
recommendations.addresses_gap: Limited legislative activity, low public-sector AI deployment, weak international governance participation, low public AI literacy
recommendations.cost_rationale: Budget 2024 committed $2.4B AI package including $100M NRC AI Assist, $50M workforce, $50M Canadian AI Safety Institute. Pan-Canadian AI Strategy Phase 2 allocated $160M talent/research, $48M knowledge mobilization, $40M compute. UK AI Research Resource received £300M — close analogue. This envelope excludes infrastructure (handled by Sovereign Compute Strategy).
responsible_agencies: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED) — lead
key_components: Sector-specific AI regulations and legislative expansion
Public-sector AI procurement scale-up and pilot deployment
Increased participation in international AI governance frameworks (target: OECD, UN, G7, new frameworks)
National AI literacy program — target move from 41st to top 25 globally (KPMG ranking)
success_metrics: Number of AI-related legislative frameworks enacted
AI procurement value and number of government deployments
Number of additional international AI governance frameworks joined
Canada's AI literacy global ranking (KPMG) — target: top 25

recommendations.title: Translational AI R&D Mission Stream
recommendations.addresses_gap: Canada's failure to move AI projects through TRL 4-8 stages; 1 notable model vs USA's 40; patent output 30x below USA
recommendations.cost_rationale: Mitacs currently receives $166M+ in annual federal support. Total federal expenses 2024-25 were $547.3B. This stream would be carved from a Pan-Canadian AI Strategy renewal envelope or incremental ISED appropriation.
recommendations.timeline: 6-9 months design, Year 1 first proposals, Years 2-3 pilot portfolio, End Year 3 evaluation and scale
responsible_agencies: CIFAR and three National AI Institutes — delivery partners
Health Canada / CIHR / NRC — health and translational-science streams
Treasury Board Secretariat — where federal science departments are involved
focus_domains: AI for health and clinical care
AI for drug discovery and life sciences
AI for advanced materials and clean technology
AI for public-sector science and decision support
recommendations.consortium_requirements: At least one university/national AI institute + one industry/startup partner + one health-system/hospital partner (for health stream)
recommendations.eligible_project_trl: TRL 4-8 (mid-to-late innovation stages — validation, prototyping, pilot deployment)
success_metrics: Primary: number of translational AI projects reaching pilot deployment or regulatory validation
AI patents filed by Canadian researchers and firms
Number of Canadian-owned AI models or products launched
Follow-on private investment raised by funded consortia

recommendations.title: National AI Adoption and Productivity Fund (with Mitacs-IRAP AI Talent Platform)
recommendations.addresses_gap: 12% Canadian business AI adoption rate; no Canadian cluster in global top 25; firms citing compute and talent barriers
recommendations.adoption_target: Increase business AI adoption from 12% to 25%

mitacs_irap_talent_platform.description: AI-specific enhancement to existing Mitacs Accelerate and IRAP programs
mitacs_irap_talent_platform.rationale: Mitacs delivered 20,885 internships through 7,202 projects in 2024-25. Participating firms saw avg 9% revenue gain, 16% sales gain, 11% labour productivity gain by year 3. However program is too broad, not AI-specific, SME absorptive capacity weak, review times slow.
components: AI readiness advisory service for SMEs
Fast-track 2-week review pathway for AI projects
Standardized AI IP framework for collaborations
mitacs_irap_talent_platform.cost: $18-40 million per year, plus $500K-1M one-time for IP framework

sovereign_compute_component.description: Sectoral Compute Access and Model Development Window added to existing Sovereign AI Compute Strategy
sovereign_compute_component.cost: $50-100 million per year within the broader compute envelope (3-year pilot)
sectors: medical imaging and clinical AI models
tiers: Tier 1: university and institute-led model research
Tier 3: translational consortia in high-priority sectors

success_metrics: Business AI adoption rate increase toward 25% target
Number of Canadian-developed AI models advanced to pilot or commercial stage
Compute utilization by Canadian startups and researchers
Patents and publications generated per compute allocation

recommendation_1.lead_agencies: ISED, Treasury Board, Global Affairs, CIFAR
recommendation_1.primary_metric: AI literacy ranking, legislative frameworks, procurement value

recommendation_2.primary_metric: Translational projects reaching pilot/validation; patents filed

recommendation_3.primary_metric: AI adoption rate (12% to 25%); sector-specific adoption growth

collective_impact: The three recommendations address sequential stages of Canada's translation gap: Recommendation 1 strengthens governance foundation and public trust; Recommendation 2 accelerates mid-stage R&D translation; Recommendation 3 drives demand-side adoption and economic impact. Together they address why Canada has strong research supply but weak commercial outcomes.
